question_text: Which of the following statements correctly compares voltage in series and parallel circuits?

options.label: A
options.text: Voltage is the same across all components in both series and parallel circuits

options.label: B
options.text: In series circuits, voltage is shared between components; in parallel circuits, voltage is the same across all branches

options.label: C
options.text: In parallel circuits, voltage is shared between components; in series circuits, voltage is the same across all resistors

options.label: D
options.text: Voltage is always divided equally between components in series circuits

explanation: In series circuits, the voltage from the power supply is shared among all components, so voltages add up. In parallel circuits, each branch receives the full voltage from the power supply, so voltage is the same everywhere.
